title: How to Bake Sourdough Scones on a Silicone Mat for Tea Time
keyword: bake sourdough scones silicone mat tea time
meta_description: Learn how to bake sourdough scones on a silicone mat for tea time. Get a golden-brown crust and zero-scrub cleanup with our expert baking tips.
html:
There is something deeply satisfying about using sourdough discard for a batch of scones. It adds a complex, tangy depth that balances perfectly with clotted cream and jam. However, the biggest hurdle with scones—especially those with a higher butter content—is the bottom. Many bakers struggle with scones that stick to the pan or burn on the bottom before the centers are set. That is why I always bake sourdough scones on a silicone mat for tea time; it ensures a consistent, golden-brown finish without the stress of scrubbing a baking sheet.

Why Use Silicone Baking Mats for Sourdough Scones?

If you are still tearing off sheets of parchment paper, you are missing out on a more consistent bake. I recommend using Silicone Baking Mats because they act as a thermal buffer between the metal pan and your dough. Metal conducts heat rapidly, which can lead to "hot spots" that char the bottom of your scones while the tops remain pale.

The non-stick surface of these mats is a game-changer for sourdough recipes. Sourdough discard can sometimes be stickier than standard flour mixes due to the fermentation. With a high-quality mat, the scones slide right off once they've cooled slightly, preserving that delicate, flaky crust you worked so hard to create.

The Secret to Flaky Sourdough Scones

The key to a great scone is keeping your fats cold. When you mix your cold butter into the flour and sourdough discard, you want small, pea-sized chunks of butter to remain. These chunks steam in the oven, creating those signature airy layers.

Avoid overworking the dough. Mix until the ingredients are just combined. If you knead the dough like bread, you'll develop too much gluten, resulting in a tough, bready scone rather than a tender, crumbly one. Gently pat the dough into a disc about an inch thick before cutting your circles.

How to Bake Sourdough Scones on a Silicone Mat for Tea Time

Once your scones are cut and chilled, it's time to prep the oven. Preheat to 400°F. Line your heavy-duty baking sheet with Silicone Baking Mats. This setup provides the perfect environment for even heat distribution.

Follow these steps for the best results:

  • Space them out: Leave at least two inches between each scone to allow hot air to circulate. This ensures the sides get a light golden crisp.
  • Brush with cream: For that professional tea-room look, brush the tops with heavy cream and sprinkle with coarse sugar.
  • Bake and Rotate: Bake for 15-20 minutes. If your oven has a known hot spot, rotate the pan halfway through.
  • The Cool Down: Let the scones rest on the mat for 5 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack. This prevents the bottoms from becoming soggy.

Cleaning Up After Your Tea Time Bake

One of the best parts of using Silicone Baking Mats is the cleanup. We've all been there: a piece of parchment paper tears, or worse, a bit of butter leaks and burns onto the baking sheet, requiring an hour of scrubbing with steel wool.

With silicone, there is zero-scrub cleanup. Any stray bits of flour or butter wipe away with a damp cloth and mild soap. Because the mats protect the pan, your baking sheets stay pristine, extending their lifespan and saving you a massive amount of effort in the kitchen.

Pairing Your Scones for the Perfect Spread

Now that you've mastered the bake, it's time for the presentation. Sourdough scones pair wonderfully with traditional English accompaniments. I recommend a thick layer of salted butter or clotted cream, topped with a dollop of raspberry or apricot preserves.

For a modern twist, try serving them with a lemon-curd glaze. The brightness of the lemon cuts through the richness of the butter and complements the tang of the sourdough discard perfectly. Serve them warm, straight from the mat to the plate, alongside a pot of Earl Grey or a strong English Breakfast tea.

FAQ

Can I use silicone mats with a convection oven?

Yes, silicone mats are excellent for convection ovens. They help prevent the bottom of the scones from browning too quickly while the fan circulates heat around the tops, resulting in a very even bake.

Do I need to grease the mat before adding the scones?

No, you do not need to grease Silicone Baking Mats. They are naturally non-stick, so your sourdough scones will release easily without adding extra fat to the recipe.

Why are my sourdough scones spreading too much on the mat?

This usually happens if the butter has softened too much. Ensure your butter is ice-cold, and try chilling your cut scones in the fridge for 15 minutes before placing them on the mat and putting them in the oven.
